text: The 'terser rough serialization' unblock for q-0008 cannot be done cheaply: it is NOT a formatting problem. Re-rendering the rough term-mode completions with the most aggressive cosmetic terseness possible by text transform (strip universe annotations, drop @ sigils, short-name every dotted chain, single-line) removes at most ~56% of chars at the median and still leaves rough a median 68x longer than fine (raw 154x), with 0% of the 607 pairs reaching a <=2x length ratio and only ~4% of rough completions falling below even the fine MAX. The 144x confound is structural to term-mode-vs-tactic serialization -- it lives in the explicit elaborated type/instance arguments, which only real re-elaboration (pp.explicit false / a delaborator on Modal) can hide, not in removable whitespace/qualifier verbosity. The length-matched-subset alternative is likewise near-empty. So q-0008 stays non-viable on the current corpus: closing the length gap requires re-extracting rough proofs with a genuinely terser (re-elaborated) rendering -- a real pipeline change, not a post-processing step -- and even that is not guaranteed to reach common support.
